concerns.text: FR-001 (Ingest real data) and US-1 (Ingest raw data) lack a task to implement the actual fetcher for a real dataset if one is found. T051 describes the logic ('IF T049a found... implement specific, verified data fetcher') but does not define a distinct task to write the fetcher code for a specific ID (e.g., GEO GSE). If T049a returns 'None Found', T051 halts, but if it returns an ID, the implementation of the fetcher is implied within T051's conditional logic rather than being a standalone, testable task. This risks the 'real data' path being unimplemented if the 'None Found' path is taken first.

FR-007 (6-hour execution limit) and SC-004 (measure execution time) are not covered by a task that actually runs the full pipeline on a dataset large enough to stress the 6-hour limit. T063a simulates a large dataset but verifies 'streaming logic' and 'performance report', not the actual 6-hour constraint on a real or large proxy dataset. T066 runs on 'real data (if available)', but if T051 fails (no data), T066 is skipped, leaving the 6-hour constraint unverified on any substantial data volume.

FR-001 (Ingest real data) and US-1 (Ingest raw data) are not covered by a task to implement the 'multi-cohort harmonization' ingestion logic described in T068. T068 describes the goal ('Implement a data harmonization script...') but does not explicitly tag it as covering FR-001 or US-1. While T069 're-enables' the pipeline, the actual implementation of the harmonization ingestion (the core of FR-001 for this scenario) is only implied in T068's description without a clear [FR-001] or [US-1] tag, making traceability ambiguous.

Task T026b ('Verify T026 execution is blocked...') references a dependency on T016 and T025 artifacts. However, T016 is in Phase 3 (US1) and T025 is in Phase 4 (US2). The task description implies a verification of orchestration logic, but it is unclear if this is a test task or an implementation task. It lacks a clear [US#] tag or [P] marker, and its description ('Verify T026 execution is blocked') sounds like a test, yet it is listed under 'Implementation'. This ambiguity makes it difficult to map to a specific FR/SC.

Task T058_new and T059 both describe 'RAM check' logic. T058_new says 'If estimated usage > 7GB, raise SystemExit'. T059 says 'switches to streaming mode if > 6GB'. These are contradictory logic paths for the same constraint (7GB vs 6GB) and outcome (Exit vs Stream). While T059 notes 'Streaming is only allowed if T058_new passes', the thresholds (7GB vs 6GB) are inconsistent. This is a writing/consistency issue in the task description that needs clarification to ensure the FR-007 (6-hour limit) and Assumption-001 (7GB RAM) are correctly implemented.
